task: Write a Python function median(nums) returning the middle value of the sorted list when the length is odd, or the average of the two middle values as a float when the length is even. An empty list returns None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The solution correctly handles the edge case of an empty list.
trace: The function `median` first checks if the input list `nums` is empty, returning `None` as specified for this edge case. If not empty, it sorts the list and calculates the middle index. Depending on whether the length of the list is odd or even, it returns the middle element or the average of the two middle elements, respectively. This approach ensures the function handles both typical cases and the specified edge case concisely and correctly.
